nodes.name: Significant increase in dry leaf biomass

source_spans.text: application of a 20:20:20 (N:P:K) water-soluble fertilizer produced a significant increase in the dry leaf biomass

nodes.name: Significant increase in total leaf area

source_spans.text: application of a 20:20:20 (N:P:K) water-soluble fertilizer produced a significant increase in the dry leaf biomass, total leaf area, and total dry biomass

nodes.name: Maximized emergence, growth, and survivorship of seedlings

nodes.name: Larger total leaf area in full sunlight

source_spans.text: Seedlings growing in full sunlight had significantly larger total leaf area per plant
Seedlings growing in full sunlight had significantly larger total leaf area per plant... than those growing in the shade
seedlings grown in the shade exhibited lower survival
in large amounts it inhibited both emergence and survival

nodes.name: Decrease in length of the longest leaf

source_spans.text: mean length of the longest leaf per plant showed a significant decrease over control within one week of both the 25 and 50% herbivory treatments
one of the leading causes of C. pitcheri mortality has been herbivory by white-tailed deer

nodes.name: Stimulated plant growth from small amounts of burial

source_spans.text: In small amounts, it stimulated plant growth

nodes.name: Inhibited emergence from large amounts of burial

source_spans.text: in large amounts it inhibited both emergence and survival
application of additional nutrients improved the growth of plants

nodes.name: Detrimental growth from slow release fertilizer pellets

source_spans.text: slow release fertilizer pellets were found to be detrimental to both the growth and biomass of C. pitcheri

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:db458ad1ae85
edges.original_sentence: Sand burial experiments showed that greenhouse grown plants should optimally be transplanted into areas receiving 5 cm of sand deposition: burial at this depth maximized emergence, survivorship, and below-ground biomass.

source_spans.text: Sand burial experiments showed that greenhouse grown plants should optimally be transplanted into areas receiving 5 cm of sand deposition: burial at this depth maximized emergence, survivorship, and below-ground biomass.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:9cb9cbb4cd1b
edges.original_sentence: Seedlings growing in full sunlight had significantly larger total leaf area per plant, root length, length of the longest leaf, number of leaves per plant, and total dry biomass of leaves, roots, and the whole plant than those growing in the shade

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:ca71b0e362fb
